vulnerablePopulations: Rural men with delayed healthcare access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: USI guidelines, DRE, PSA, IPSS, uroflowmetry

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: USI criteria, DRE primary in rural areas, PSA/TRUS in urban centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Medical: tamsulosin, finasteride; Surgical: TURP, limited HoLEP; Ayurvedic: Gokshura, Shilajit for urinary symptoms
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited urologist access (<1 per 100,000), expensive PSA testing, late presentation, cultural reluctance to discuss urinary symptoms
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic alpha-blockers/5-ARIs, USI guidelines, Ayurvedic urinary tonics, growing TURP access
